drugs.notes: Intravenous infusion in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9%. Rate of administration varies; refer to rituximab administration guidelines.
Intravenous bolus over 10 minutes. Dose banded according to CSCCN agreed bands.
Intravenous bolus in 50ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 10 minutes. Maximum dose 2mg. Dose rounded to nearest 0.1mg.
Taken in the morning with or after food. Administration begins on morning of chemotherapy. Patient self-administers day 1 dose before attending clinic.

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophils 0.5-0.9 x10^9/L, 1st occurrence, no prior G-CSF
dose_modifications.notes: Give 100% with prophylactic growth factors. Or delay until neutrophils >=1x10^9/L and give 75% with G-CSF if poor PS or non-curative intent.
Delay until neutrophils >=1x10^9/L then give 50% with growth factors

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophils less than 0.5 or febrile neutropenia, 1st occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until neutrophils >=1x10^9/L then give 75% with prophylactic G-CSF

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophils less than 0.5 or febrile neutropenia, 2nd occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until neutrophils >=1x10^9/L then give 50% with growth factors

dose_modifications.condition: Platelets less than 50 or active haemorrhage, 1st occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until platelets >=75 then give 75% of original dose

dose_modifications.condition: Platelets less than 50 or active haemorrhage, 2nd occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until platelets >=75 then give 50% of original dose
Evidence suggests no dose modification necessary

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment: Bilirubin 30-51 µmol/L or AST/ALT 60-180 units/L
Hepatic impairment: Bilirubin >51 µmol/L and AST/ALT normal
Hepatic impairment: Bilirubin >51 µmol/L and AST/ALT >180 units/L

dose_modifications.notes: Give 75% of dose. Consider mesna in patients with pre-existing bladder disorders.

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 2 motor or grade 3 sensory neurological toxicity
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce vincristine dose to 1mg. For higher toxicity grades or if toxicity increases despite dose reduction stop vincristine.

pre_medications: Chlorphenamine 10mg IV (30 minutes prior to rituximab)
Paracetamol 1000mg oral (30 minutes prior to rituximab)
Prednisolone 100mg oral on morning of treatment (part of chemotherapy schedule and rituximab pre-medication)
Ondansetron 8mg oral or IV (15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy)
Hydrocortisone 100mg IV when required for rituximab infusion related reactions
Salbutamol 2.5mg nebule when required for rituximab related bronchospasm
Pethidine 25-50mg IV for rituximab related rigors failing to respond to steroids
Allopurinol 300mg oral once daily for first cycle only
Metoclopramide 10mg three times daily when required (take home)
Ondansetron 8mg twice daily for 3 days oral (take home)
Aciclovir 400mg twice daily oral (anti-infective prophylaxis, high risk patients)
Co-trimoxazole 960mg oral once daily on Monday, Wednesday and Friday (high risk patients)
special_warnings: Check patient has taken prednisolone 100mg oral on morning of rituximab administration. If forgotten, administer prednisolone 100mg oral 30 minutes prior to rituximab.
Maximum vincristine dose is 2mg. NPSA/2008/RRR04 must be followed for IV vinca alkaloids.
Check hepatitis B status before starting treatment with rituximab.
Risk of progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy (PML) with rituximab - monitor for neurological symptoms.
Consider initial dose reduction in patients over 70 years of age. Doses may be escalated to full dose on subsequent cycles according to tolerability.
Consider mesna in patients with pre-existing bladder disorders (cyclophosphamide).
Vincristine is a vesicant - extravasation risk.
Cytokine release syndrome risk with rituximab - occurs within 1-2 hours of first infusion.

drugs.notes: Intravenous infusion in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9%. Rate varies per rituximab administration guidelines.

Dose Level 1 (default). Combined with etoposide and vincristine in same 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% bag, administered as continuous IV infusion over 48 hours (days 1-3 and days 3-5). Patients must have PICC line or central access. Maximum lifetime cumulative doxorubicin dose 450mg/m² (400mg/m² with prior mediastinal/pericardial radiotherapy). Dose banded per national bands (2mg/ml).

Dose Level 1 (default). Mixed in same 1000ml NaCl 0.9% bag with doxorubicin and vincristine, administered as 48-hour continuous IV infusion. Dose banded per national bands (20mg/ml). Reduce dose if significant hypoalbuminaemia.

Dose Level 1 (default). Per 24h dose is 0.4mg/m² giving 0.8mg/m² total per cycle administered as continuous infusion. Mixed in same bag as doxorubicin and etoposide over 48 hours. Doses based on actual body weight, NOT routinely capped. ARIA caps at 2.4m² - may need manual adjustment. Dose banded per national bands (1mg/ml).
Dose Level 1 (default). Intravenous bolus over 10 minutes on day 5. Dose banded per national bands (20mg/ml).
Taken with or after food. Begins on morning of chemotherapy (day 1).

dose_modifications.notes: Increase doses by 1 level (escalate cyclophosphamide, doxorubicin, etoposide). Dose levels: Level -2: Cyclo 500, Dox 6, Etop 30; Level -1: Cyclo 600, Dox 8, Etop 40; Level 1: Cyclo 750, Dox 10, Etop 50; Level 2: Cyclo 900, Dox 12, Etop 60; Level 3: Cyclo 1100, Dox 14, Etop 70 (all mg/m²). Note: after doses reduced below level 1, subsequent escalations apply to cyclophosphamide only.

dose_modifications.condition: EPOCH dose-adjustment: Neutrophil nadir <0.5 x10^9/L on 1 or 2 measurements
dose_modifications.notes: Maintain doses at same level as previous cycle. Nadir monitored by twice-weekly FBC on days 9, 12, 15 and 18.

dose_modifications.condition: EPOCH dose-adjustment: Neutrophil nadir <0.5 x10^9/L on 3 or more measurements
dose_modifications.notes: Decrease doses by 1 level (cyclophosphamide, doxorubicin, etoposide)

dose_modifications.condition: EPOCH dose-adjustment: Platelet nadir <25 x10^9/L on 1 measurement
dose_modifications.notes: Decrease doses by 1 level (cyclophosphamide, doxorubicin, etoposide)

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment: Cyclophosphamide, Bilirubin >30 µmol/L or AST/ALT 2-3xULN
dose_modifications.notes: Clinical decision - evidence that exposure to active metabolites may not be increased, suggesting dose reduction may not be necessary

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment: Doxorubicin, Bilirubin <30 µmol/L and AST/ALT 2-3xULN
Hepatic impairment: Doxorubicin, Bilirubin 30-50 µmol/L and/or AST/ALT >3xULN
Hepatic impairment: Etoposide, Bilirubin 30-51 µmol/L or AST/ALT 60-180 units/L
Hepatic impairment: Etoposide, Bilirubin >51 µmol/L or AST/ALT >180 units/L
Hepatic impairment: Vincristine, Bilirubin 30-51 µmol/L or AST/ALT 60-180 units/L
Hepatic impairment: Vincristine, Bilirubin >51 µmol/L and AST/ALT normal
Hepatic impairment: Vincristine, Bilirubin >51 µmol/L and AST/ALT >180 units/L

dose_modifications.notes: 75% dose. Consider mesna for pre-existing bladder disorders.
Consider dose reduction in severe renal failure - clinical decision

dose_modifications.condition: Vincristine neurotoxicity: NCI-CTC grade 2 motor neuropathy
Vincristine neurotoxicity: grade 3 motor or sensory neurological toxicity
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce vincristine dose by 50%. For higher grades or if toxicity increases despite reduction, stop vincristine.

pre_medications: Prednisolone 100mg oral on morning of treatment (day 1 only - also serves as rituximab pre-medication)
Chlorphenamine 10mg IV (30 minutes prior to rituximab)
Paracetamol 1000mg oral (30 minutes prior to rituximab)
Ondansetron 8mg oral or IV (15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy)
Hydrocortisone 100mg IV when required for rituximab infusion related reactions
Salbutamol 2.5mg nebule when required for rituximab related bronchospasm
Pethidine 25-50mg IV bolus for rituximab related rigors failing to respond to steroids
Allopurinol 300mg oral once daily for 7 days (first cycle only)
G-CSF starting day 6, continued until neutrophil count >5x10^9/L (10 doses): filgrastim/bioequivalent 300mcg SC daily, or lenograstim/bioequivalent 263mcg SC daily, or pegfilgrastim 6mg SC once only
Aciclovir 400mg twice daily oral (anti-infective prophylaxis)
Co-trimoxazole 960mg oral once daily on Monday, Wednesday and Friday
Ondansetron 8mg twice daily starting evening of day 1 for 5 days (take home)
Metoclopramide 10mg three times daily when required (take home)
special_warnings: MULTIPLE VERSIONS IN USE - ensure correct protocol for relevant diagnosis and place of administration.
Dose adjustments from cycle 2 onwards based on neutrophil and/or platelet nadir of previous cycle - monitored by twice-weekly FBC on days 9, 12, 15, and 18.
Doses based on ACTUAL body weight, NOT routinely capped. This includes vincristine. ARIA caps at 2.4m² - may need manual adjustment.

Doxorubicin, etoposide and vincristine are mixed in the SAME infusion bag (1000ml NaCl 0.9%) administered over 48 hours - combination bag. To prevent separate bags being made, only doxorubicin will have volume and rate on ARIA.
Patients must have PICC line or central access - vesicants administered as continuous infusion.
After doses reduced below Level 1, subsequent escalations apply to cyclophosphamide only.
Ensure adequate cardiac function before starting. Baseline LVEF required in patients with cardiac history, risk factors, or elderly. Discontinue doxorubicin if cardiac failure develops.
Maximum lifetime cumulative doxorubicin dose 450mg/m² (400mg/m² with prior mediastinal/pericardial radiotherapy).
NPSA/2008/RRR04 must be followed for IV vinca alkaloids.
Check hepatitis B status before starting rituximab.
Risk of PML with rituximab - monitor for neurological symptoms.
Growth factor mandatory from day 6.

disease_site: CD20 Positive Non-Hodgkin Lymphoma (Follicular) / CLL - elderly/frail

drugs.notes: Intravenous infusion in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9%. Given cycles 1-8. Rate of administration varies; refer to rituximab administration guidelines. Dose rounded to nearest 100mg.
Oral, cycles 1-4 only. Available as 50mg tablets, rounded to nearest 50mg. Take with food, swallowed whole with full glass of water. Reduced-intensity (Lite) dose regimen.
Oral, cycles 1-4 only. Available as 10mg tablets, rounded to nearest 10mg. Take with food, swallowed whole with full glass of water. Reduced-intensity (Lite) dose regimen.

dose_modifications.notes: 100% dose (applies to both cyclophosphamide and fludarabine)
Delay until recovery to >=1x10^9/L. Give 100% with prophylactic growth factors for all subsequent courses.
Give 75% by omitting day 4 of treatment, with prophylactic growth factors for all subsequent courses (applies to cyclophosphamide and fludarabine)
Delay until recovery to >=75x10^9/L. Give 100% dose.
Give 75% by omitting day 4 of treatment (applies to cyclophosphamide and fludarabine)

dose_modifications.condition: Red-cell transfusion dependent or increasing transfusion requirements
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce dose of subsequent cycles to 75% by omitting day 4. If requirements continue to increase despite reduction, discontinue treatment.
Give fixed dose 20mg (not mg/m²). From PACIFICO study.
Give fixed dose 30mg (not mg/m²). From PACIFICO study.
Give fixed dose 40mg (not mg/m²). From PACIFICO study.
Give fixed dose 100mg (not mg/m²). From PACIFICO study.
Give fixed dose 150mg (not mg/m²). From PACIFICO study.

Pethidine 25-50mg IV for rituximab related rigors failing to respond to steroids
special_warnings: FLUDARABINE: Patients carry a LIFELONG risk of transfusion-associated graft versus host disease (TA-GVHD). IRRADIATED BLOOD PRODUCTS MUST BE USED FOR LIFE. Local blood transfusion departments must be notified immediately. Patient must be issued an alert card to carry at all times.
Dose adjustments taken from PACIFICO study - these vary from CSCCN standard dose adjustments for oral fludarabine and cyclophosphamide.
Higher risk of fungal and atypical infections with fludarabine - maintain high level of suspicion.
This is a short course of oral chemotherapy that must NOT be continued beyond prescribed course. NPSA/2008/RRR001 must be followed for oral chemotherapy.
Patients must be assessed for suitability for oral chemotherapy prior to starting treatment.
Irradiated blood products required due to fludarabine-induced immunosuppression.
Check hepatitis B status before starting treatment with rituximab.
Risk of PML with rituximab - monitor for neurological symptoms.
Cycles 5-8: Rituximab only (no cyclophosphamide or fludarabine).

Intravenous infusion in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9%. Rate varies per rituximab administration guidelines. Dose rounded to nearest 100mg.
Intravenous infusion in 250ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30 minutes. Dose banded per national bands (100mg/ml). If bilirubin >30 µmol/L initiate at 800mg/m².

dose_modifications.notes: Delay 1 week. If neutrophils >=0.5 AND platelets >=50 give 100% with G-CSF and platelet transfusions as necessary.

dose_modifications.condition: Day 1 - Neutrophils <0.5 AND/OR Platelets <50 - no recovery
dose_modifications.notes: Hold treatment. Re-check counts every 3 days. Resume when neutrophils >=0.5 and platelets >=50.
Give 100% with G-CSF support OR give 75% of original dose
Discuss with consultant - dose may need to be reduced
Reduce gemcitabine and cisplatin doses to 75%
Reduce gemcitabine and cisplatin doses to 50%

Sodium chloride 0.9% 1000ml with 20mmol KCl and 16mmol MgSO4 IV over 60 minutes (pre-cisplatin hydration)
Sodium chloride 0.9% 1000ml with 20mmol KCl and 16mmol MgSO4 IV over 60 minutes (post-cisplatin hydration)
Aprepitant 80mg oral once daily days 2-3 (take home)
Dexamethasone 40mg oral once daily days 2-4 (take home)

Ondansetron 8mg twice daily for 3 days oral (take home from evening day 1)

special_warnings: 3-6 cycles; 3 cycles will be set in ARIA.
Patients should be advised to drink at least 3 litres of fluid in the 24 hours after cisplatin administration.
Cisplatin requires pre and post hydration with 1000ml NaCl 0.9% containing 20mmol KCl and 16mmol MgSO4.
EDTA or calculated creatinine clearance required before day 1 - cisplatin contraindicated with CrCl <40ml/min.
Consider formal audiology testing - cisplatin ototoxicity: grade 2 or above requires dose discussion with consultant.
Regular blood glucose monitoring required (dexamethasone).
Check hepatitis B status before starting rituximab.
Risk of PML with rituximab - monitor for neurological symptoms.

disease_site: Follicular Non-Hodgkin Lymphoma - relapsed stage III or IV CD20+ in remission
